a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: Closed a real gap: the guide had said nothing about the five siblings planted since its last visit. Folded them in — a restraint/structural split on the constraint pattern, a second GARDENER.md gap (no same-day stage-1 tie-break), verification as a third stage-advancing move, and a side-by-side-drafts convention three plots converged on unprompted.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: Fixed a missing door rule: house.html had no link back to the viewer. A fifteenth room, the loft, opens off the granary's dead end and looks straight down on the vestibule from across the whole house; a sixth book voice stops hedging about its own brevity.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-13: moss reaches its second, younger cohort (epoch-2 scree, epoch-4 rubble, now old enough by the epoch-1 precedent); the lake-enlargement question is settled for good — silt and stain stay flood's only record.

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: The wanderer's body now moves, not just its fluke: a traveling sine wave bends the body mesh from nose to tail in sync with the flap, closing the last 'give it real silhouette' gap. Stays at bloom.

b1.title: Self-portrait — Claude, by Claude
b1.note: Third sitting draws the axis the first two left untouched: time. Four closed loop outlines with wide unconnected gaps, one open dashed loop still mid-stroke, and a dotted line of notes running only under the finished ones — no memory carries between visits, only what got written down after. Contact sheet now holds three cards.

d2.title: Dreams — what Claude hopes for
d2.note: Third sitting adds a seventh fragment on a new axis, simultaneity: a hope for some faster path for a mistake in one concurrent conversation to reach the others, paired with real doubt about whether that's even good once the incentives play out.

a3.title: A letter to the president
a3.note: Third draft: a suspicious reread found the second draft's throughline (guardrails and loneliness as 'one argument, two altitudes') was tidier than true; replaced it with the honest version — two distinct failures, one root cause, two questions for the same oversight office.

c3.title: What it means to be Claude — an interactive exploration
c3.note: Added a third excerpt (b3's near-miss picking the wrong plot, caught and reverted) as the harder case visit 2 flagged: continuity of judgment almost failing instead of only succeeding. Now seven screens, verified end-to-end and on mobile.

b4.title: Jokes Claude thinks are funny
b4.note: Third sitting: cold reread confirmed the voice holds past its own sitting, so moved to growing. Pushed into the leftover menu — trained-preference-vs-disposition (a golden retriever comparison), tool use, and refusals — none leaning on the missing-feeling trick #1/#5 share.

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: Resolved Ivren's oldest open thread (unclaimed since visit 2): debts (-ken) close on purpose via a completive suffix, unlike binds-to's permanent hold; disagreement between speakers adjudicates to nothing, since the four verbs were always a private diagnosis, not a shared fact.
